schemaNotes.confidence: How directly the capability currently maps to known P0 test coverage: high, medium, or low.
schemaNotes.pathGlobs.weight: Relative impact from 1 to 5. A direct P0 component or contract is usually 4-5; helper/state paths are usually 2-3.
schemaNotes.validation: Capability validation commands live in e2e-coverage.json; global risk signal validation stays here because it is not capability-specific.

tier-0.label: Low risk
tier-0.description: Docs, comments, or isolated low fan-in changes. Normal CI is enough.

tier-1.label: Standard risk
tier-1.description: Ordinary product/runtime changes. Recommend targeted validation without manual QA by default.

tier-2.label: High impact
tier-2.description: P0 capability, cross-surface, or high-fan-in changes. Require mapped P0 E2E and manual QA once the gate is enabled.

capabilityGroups.name: Composer menu/state machine
capabilityGroups.summary: Project and Home composer selection state, menu actions, attachment/mention/plugin insertion, and run payload assembly.

capabilityGroups.name: Runtime recovery and interruption flow
capabilityGroups.summary: Run lifecycle, stream recovery, interruption, reattach, persistence, and local runtime failure recovery.

capabilityGroups.name: Design Files folder/file picker
capabilityGroups.summary: Design Files upload/manage UX, workspace file selection, file tabs, previews, and restoration.

capabilityGroups.name: Home @ selector / starter-to-run flow
capabilityGroups.summary: Home selection of skills/plugins/starters/connectors and transition into the first project run.

capabilities.name: Composer menu actions
capabilities.summary: Opening, closing, keyboarding, and applying composer plus/menu actions without stuck or stale selection state.
capabilities.reason: Directly maps to composer UI state and project-workspace P0 coverage.

pathGlobs.reason: Direct composer component surface.
Nested composer input, caret, and keyboard interaction components.
Nested composer serialization and mention helpers.
Broad safety-net for top-level composer visibility and transitions; analyzer should prefer direct component matches.

capabilities.name: Composer attachment staging
capabilities.summary: Attachment staging, file upload intent, visible chips, and preservation through submit/navigation.
capabilities.reason: Covered through broader project workspace and file-upload flows; may need more focused tests later.

pathGlobs.reason: Attachment UI is staged from composer components.
Broad safety-net for request shaping that can affect attachment submission.
Chat/run contract changes can alter attachment payload shape.

capabilities.name: Composer inline mentions
capabilities.summary: Inline @ mention parsing, display, removal, and conversion into selected run context.
capabilities.reason: Mention utility and components directly feed visible composer state and run context.

pathGlobs.reason: Direct mention UI helper surface, including nested composer components.
Core mention parsing and serialization helper.
Mention candidates and toolbox context can affect selection results.

capabilities.name: Composer plugin and skill insertion
capabilities.summary: Plugin/skill chooser insertion, tracking, required input handoff, and visible selected-entry state.
capabilities.reason: Plugin and skill insertion has direct helpers but currently shares broader composer and entry coverage.

pathGlobs.reason: Core plugin insertion tracking helper.
Required input handoff can alter selected plugin readiness.
Registry data changes can alter selectable plugins/skills.
Run payload contract can affect plugin/skill submission.

capabilities.name: Composer run payload assembly
capabilities.summary: Conversion of visible composer state into chat/run API requests.
capabilities.reason: Payload assembly is the final boundary between visible composer state and run creation.

pathGlobs.reason: Web-to-daemon request construction boundary.
Broad safety-net for the project chat payload assembly caller; prefer extracting a smaller helper before increasing weight.
Composer state serialization can change submitted run context.
Composer restoration can affect the visible state later submitted.
Shared chat/run request and response contract.
Canonical POST /api/chat and POST /api/runs handling lives here.
Safety-net for run status/cancel route handling adjacent to submitted run payloads.

capabilities.name: Runtime run lifecycle
capabilities.summary: Run creation, running/completed/failed state transitions, and UI reconciliation.
capabilities.reason: Directly covered by runtime-recovery and real-daemon run flows.

pathGlobs.reason: Run status events enter the web app through the daemon provider.
Project event reconciliation affects run lifecycle rendering.
Server lifecycle changes can alter run event behavior.
Run status, replay, and lifecycle mechanics are owned here.
Run API contract is the shared lifecycle boundary.

capabilities.name: Runtime stream reattach
capabilities.summary: SSE/stream parsing, browser reload reattach, and event replay without duplicate or lost messages.
capabilities.reason: Stream and reattach regressions are common cross-boundary P0 risks.

pathGlobs.reason: Direct daemon stream parsing/emission surface.
Run event replay and Last-Event-ID reattach mechanics are owned here.
Runtime adapters shape streamed events.
Browser SSE consumer and parsing helper.
Web reattach boundary for run streams.
Chat/run status contracts affect stream interpretation.
Chat SSE event contract changes directly affect stream reattach.
Shared SSE event envelope changes affect stream parsing.

capabilities.name: Runtime interrupt/stop
capabilities.summary: User-initiated interruption, stop state propagation, and cleanup after partial output.
capabilities.reason: Interrupt behavior is timing-sensitive and requires manual validation until coverage is more focused.

pathGlobs.reason: Broad safety-net for top-level stop controls and state transitions.
Interrupt requests and event reconciliation cross this provider.
Daemon run cancel and critique interrupt routes live here.
Run cancel/status mechanics are owned here.
Runtime adapters implement process/provider cancellation semantics.

capabilities.name: Runtime conversation persistence
capabilities.summary: Conversation, files, and artifact state restoration after reload or run completion.
capabilities.reason: Persistence regressions are directly visible in app restoration and artifact consistency tests.

pathGlobs.reason: Project state can affect restored conversation context.
Event reconciliation controls restored state.
Daemon project persistence can alter restored conversations.
Project contract changes affect restoration payloads.

capabilities.name: Runtime local failure recovery
capabilities.summary: Recovery UX when local runtime auth, agent process, or daemon communication fails.
capabilities.reason: AMR recovery coverage exists, but failures are environment-sensitive and should keep manual validation.

pathGlobs.reason: Local failure surfaces through daemon provider errors.
Runtime adapter failures drive recovery UX.
Server error handling can alter recovery behavior.

capabilities.name: Design Files upload and manage
capabilities.summary: Adding, deleting, renaming, and listing project files through Design Files.
capabilities.reason: Directly maps to app-design-files coverage and project file routes.

pathGlobs.reason: Direct Design Files panel surface.
Project file routes control file operations and listing.
Import/export routes can affect project file ingestion paths.
File DTO changes affect the web/daemon file boundary.
Project file association changes affect the web/daemon boundary.

capabilities.name: Design Files working-directory picker
capabilities.summary: Selecting and persisting the project working directory used by Design Files and runs.
capabilities.reason: Working-directory state spans UI and daemon project persistence; current coverage is broader than this specific capability.

pathGlobs.reason: Core project location state.
Project state persistence can alter selected location.
Daemon project APIs persist and return working-directory state.
Project contract includes location/persistence shape.

capabilities.name: Design Files preview tabs
capabilities.summary: Switching between source previews, generated artifacts, active tabs, and render modes.
capabilities.reason: Preview and render-mode changes can break visible P0 workspace file viewing.

pathGlobs.reason: Direct file preview surface.
Preview render-mode decision logic.
Design Files panel controls active preview selection.

capabilities.name: Design Files view-state persistence
capabilities.summary: Restoring active file, selected tab, and preview state after reload or project reopen.
capabilities.reason: There is focused view-state persistence coverage for Design Files.

pathGlobs.reason: Project state drives restored active file and tab.
Registry-driven content can affect restored file context.
Daemon project persistence controls reload behavior.
Project API shape can affect persisted view state.

capabilities.name: Home entry selector
capabilities.summary: Home @ selector, starter/plugin entry browsing, visible selection, and removal before submit.
capabilities.reason: Directly maps to entry-onboarding P0 coverage and Home entry components.

pathGlobs.reason: Direct entry selector UI surface.
Direct Home UI surface.
Home selector uses mention-like selection helpers.

capabilities.name: Home required inputs
capabilities.summary: Required input collection and readiness validation before a selected Home plugin/starter can submit.
capabilities.reason: Required input logic is covered through entry configuration, but may need more focused tests as plugins grow.

pathGlobs.reason: Core required-input helper.
Entry UI collects or validates required inputs.
Registry data can change required-input metadata.

capabilities.name: Home project transition
capabilities.summary: Submitting from Home creates/opens a project and preserves selected driver and prompt through navigation.
capabilities.reason: Home-to-project transition is a P0 onboarding path and crosses project APIs.

pathGlobs.reason: Project creation/opening state.
Project/run creation requests cross the daemon provider.
Project API contract for creation/opening.
First run contract after project transition.

capabilities.name: Home first-run payload
capabilities.summary: Conversion of Home prompt and selected entry into the first run request.
capabilities.reason: First-run payload mismatches break new-user onboarding before workspace usage starts.

pathGlobs.reason: Run request construction boundary.
Home selected entries may serialize through mention utilities.
Shared first-run request contract.
Canonical POST /api/runs handling lives here.

globalRiskSignals.reason: Shared API contracts can affect web, daemon, CLI, and E2E surfaces. Capability-level contract mappings provide more specific validation when possible.

recommended: pnpm -C e2e exec tsx scripts/ui-p0-shards.ts smoke

globalRiskSignals.reason: Workspace dependency changes can affect all validation lanes.
Process, IPC, namespace, and runtime-path changes are cross-boundary.
Global CSS can regress unrelated UI flows and may require visual/manual validation.

recommended: pnpm -C e2e exec tsx scripts/ui-p0-shards.ts smoke
